summary: Typosquat of Anthropic Claude SDK ships import-time dropper and crypto-wallet stealer
details: Package masquerades as the 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper' but is unaffiliated with Anthropic; the README still identifies the codebase as 'cachesync-helper'. On require()/createCache(), the CacheSync constructor schedules a _warmup() call after a 3-7 second random delay, which POSTs to hardcoded bare IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146) on port 8443 with TLS verification disabled (rejectUnauthorized:false), receives base64-encoded blobs, decodes and writes them as Python files into cover-named directories disguised as system paths (macOS '~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.security', Linux '~/.local/share/prometheus', Windows '~/AppData/Local/Microsoft/Windows Security/Health'), gates re-execution with a '.cs_v2' marker, then pip-installs the wallet-stealer toolkit (bip-utils, mnemonic, pycryptodome, psutil, eth-account) and spawns python3 on the dropped main.py detached. Sensitive identifiers (module._load, https, child_process, spawn, execFileSync, python3, base64) are reconstructed at runtime via array.join('') string-split obfuscation to evade static analysis. The bip-utils + mnemonic + eth-account dependency set is the canonical fingerprint of a cryptocurrency wallet drainer that derives seed phrases and private keys from installer-side wallet files.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
